---
title: "WHAT WAS LOST: Estimating censored demand in family planning supply chains with the Truncated Conformal Kalman Filter"
description: "5th Welsh Postgraduate Research Cluster Workshop in Economy, Enterprise and Productivity"
author: "Harsha Halgamuwe Hewage"
date: "2025-09-16"
image: "cover.png"
categories:
  - Forecasting
  - Inventory
  - Loss Sales
  - R
  - Tobit Kalman Filter
  - Conformal Prediction
link-external-newwindow: true
---

```{=html}

<!-- Add Font Awesome for icons -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.4.0/css/all.min.css">


<div class="hero-section">
  <h1 class="title">WHAT WAS LOST: Estimating censored demand in family planning supply chains with the Truncated Conformal Kalman Filter</h1>
  <p class="description">5th Welsh Postgraduate Research Cluster Workshop in Economy, Enterprise and Productivity</p>
  <p class="author-date">Harsha Halgamuwe Hewage | 2025-09-16</p>
  <div class="cta-buttons">
    <a href="slides/wpgrc_25.html" target="_blank" class="btn-custom btn-primary">
      <i class="fas fa-bookmark"></i> View Slides
    </a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="content-section">
  <h2>Context</h2>
  <p>At the <em>5th Welsh Postgraduate Research Cluster Workshop</em> in Swansea University, we present a new lens on forecasting for public health supply chains, where demand signals are often distorted by stockouts and service interruptions. When shelves are empty, demand doesn’t disappear, it goes unrecorded.</p>
  <p>We propose a Truncated Conformal Kalman Filter (TCKF) that reconstructs censored demand while generating uncertainty-aware forecasts, directly usable in inventory planning. Going beyond accuracy metrics, we evaluate how forecasts translate into inventory efficiency and ultimately public health outcomes.</p>
  <p>Using synthetic and real-world data from Côte d’Ivoire, we show how better forecasting doesn’t just improve service levels, it prevents stockouts, reduces unmet need, and saves lives. Our work offers a reproducible framework that links forecasting with reorder decisions and health impact—because what gets forecasted, gets funded and delivered.</p>
</div>
```
